stimulus: Sediment cores extracted from the floor of Lake Suigetsu in central Japan and dated by counting annual varves may yield a calibration of radiocarbon ages that is more precise than any previously ______blank result that, if confirmed, would refine the chronology of late-Pleistocene climate events.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. A comma correctly marks the boundary between the main clause ("Sediment cores…achieved") and the supplementary noun phrase ("a result…events") that elaborates on the implications of the new calibration.

Choice A is incorrect because a semicolon cannot join a main clause and a supplementary noun phrase. Choice B is incorrect because it creates a sentence fragment beginning with "A result." Choice D is incorrect because it produces a run-on, fusing the two parts without punctuation.
